description:
Lift `used_as_closure_target` exclusion from `parking::natively_callable`
and branch `ir_codegen::compile_closure_stub` on `callee_is_native_abi`.
With fz-ul4.29.9's spawn-wrapper in place, every closure target body
becomes free to use the typed native ABI — the wrapper handles spawn's
deferred-execution contract.

## Scope

- `parking.rs:113` — drop the `used_as_closure_target` exclusion.
  Add `used_as_closure_target` to the `reachable_as_native` gate so
  closure-only targets (never directly called, never used as cont) are
  still admitted. Update module-level docs to reflect the lift.

- `ir_codegen.rs::compile_closure_stub` — branch on
  `callee_is_native_abi`. Native branch:
    1. Load tagged captures from `closure_ptr + 24 + 8*k` (native fns
       take tagged FzValue i64 params per build_fn_signature's
       .6.2.3 convention — no kind-aware unboxing required).
    2. Forward `arg_vals` (also tagged at the CallClosure site by
       the indirect-call sig).
    3. Append `host_ctx`.
    4. `b.ins().call(native_callee_fref, &args)` (SystemV stub
       calling CallConv::Tail native — fine for plain `call`).
    5. If `cont_ptr` is null, call `runtime.halt_id(host_ctx, result)`
       and return null (top-frame halt, mirrors emit_return). Else
       store `result` to `cont_ptr + 24` (cont's slot 1, the result
       slot) and return `cont_ptr` for trampoline dispatch.

- Fixture: `closure_native_target.fz` — factory pattern
  `add_to(x, y) → fn(z) → x + y + z`, no spawn. Expectations:
  `expect_clif_excludes: fz_stub_<inner>_2: fz_alloc_frame`
  (the inner closure's stub goes native, no frame allocation).
  Result is `42`.

## Why this is now safe

Without .29.9, lifting the exclusion broke `fixture_ping_pong_via_jit_runtime`:
the child closure's body ran inside `Runtime::spawn_closure`'s
synchronous call to the stub, while main was already removed from
`rt.tasks` → `send: receiver pid 1 not in task registry`. With .29.9
in place, `spawn_closure` always invokes `fz_spawn_thunk`'s stub
(uniform-ABI), which returns a frame for trampoline dispatch. The
original closure's body — native or not — only runs later, in the
child's quantum, when the trampoline picks up the thunk frame. By
then the registry is in the right state.

## Acceptance

- `closure_native_target.fz` passes JIT/interp/AOT.
- CLIF dump for the factory's inner-closure stub contains no
  `fz_alloc_frame` call.
- 373 unit + 5 fixture-matrix tests + the two new fixtures (or
  whichever count is current at .29.9's HEAD) stay green.
- `cargo build --release` clean (warnings are errors).
- `bw show fz-ul4.29.9` shows it closed before this ticket starts.

## Blocked by

fz-ul4.29.9 (spawn-wrapper).

## Notes for execution

- The stub today already loads tagged captures and unboxes per slot
  kind for the uniform path. The native branch is simpler: just
  forward tagged values, no per-slot kind dispatch.
- The native callee's FuncId is already in scope at the stub-build
  site (`callee_func_id` parameter of `compile_closure_stub`);
  `_callee_sig` (currently unused) can be dropped from the param
  list when the assertion at line 2770 goes away.
- The interp doesn't need changes — closure-target ABI is a codegen
  concern only.
title: .29.5.1: Lift closure-target native-eligibility
